topic-map.title: Manage Cloud-Native Router Software
topic-map.shortdesc: This topic provides information on the available upgrade, downgrade and uninstall options for JCNR.

task.title: Upgrade from Cloud-Native Router Release 23.4 and Earlier

context.p: Upgrading to this release from Cloud-Native Router release 23.4 and earlier is not supported. You must uninstall your existing Cloud-Native Router release before you can install this Cloud-Native Router release. We show you how to do this below.

note.p: Starting with Cloud-Native Router release 23.2, the Cloud-Native Router license format has changed. Request a new license key from the JAL portal if your existing Cloud-Native Router release is earlier than release 23.2.

step.cmd: Save your current configuration.

substep.cmd: Save the Cloud-Native Router Helm chart values.yaml customizations that you made.
Access the cRPD pods and save the Junos cRPD CLI configuration.
substep.info: To see the set of commands used to create the current configuration,show configuration | display set
To save the configuration, use the Junos CLI save command.

step.cmd: Uninstall JCNR.
step.info: See but don't delete the jcnr namespace or the jcnr-secrets.

step.cmd: Download the <sw_package>.tar.gz tarball to the directory of your choice. See for the available package options.
Expand the downloaded package.
Change directory to the main installation directory.
step.info: If you're installing Cloud-Native Router only, then:cd Juniper_Cloud_Native_Router_<release>This directory contains the Helm chart for Cloud-Native Router only.

If you're installing Cloud-Native Router and cSRX at the same time, then: cd Juniper_Cloud_Native_Router_CSRX_<release>This directory contains the combination Helm chart for both Cloud-Native Router and cSRX.

note.p: All remaining steps in the installation assume that your current working directory is now either Juniper_Cloud_Native_Router_<release> or Juniper_Cloud_Native_Router_CSRX_<release>.

step.cmd: View the contents in the current directory.
Change to the helmchart directory and expand the Helm chart.
step.info: cd helmchartls jcnr-<release>.tgztar -xzvf jcnr-<release>.tgzls jcnr jcnr-<release>.tgzThe Helm chart is located in the jcnr directory.

step.cmd: Modify the Helm chart helmchart/jcnr/values.yaml file to match the Helm chart configuration you saved earlier.
Install the Cloud-Native Router Helm chart.
step.info: Navigate to the helmchart/jcnr directory and run the following command:helm install jcnr .

info.codeblock: NAME: jcnr LAST DEPLOYED: xxxxxxx NAMESPACE: default STATUS: deployed REVISION: 1 TEST SUITE: None

step.cmd: Check that the Cloud-Native Router Helm chart is being installed.

info.codeblock: NAME NAMESPACE REVISION UPDATED STATUS CHART APP VERSION jcnr default 1 xxxxxxx deployed jcnr-xxxxxxx xxxxxxx
info.p: If the new version of Cloud-Native Router fails to install, troubleshoot the installation as you normally do. Look at the Cloud-Native Router deployer logs and see .

step.cmd: Reconfigure cRPD with the saved Junos CLI commands.
step.info: Access the cRPD CLI and use the Junos CLI load command to load the previously saved configuration.

task.title: Upgrade from Cloud-Native Router Release 24.2 and Later

step.cmd: Download the <sw_package>.tar.gz tarball to the directory of your choice. See for the available package options.
Expand the downloaded package.
Change directory to the main installation directory.
step.info: If you're installing Cloud-Native Router only, then:cd Juniper_Cloud_Native_Router_<release>This directory contains the Helm chart for Cloud-Native Router only.

If you're installing Cloud-Native Router and cSRX at the same time, then: cd Juniper_Cloud_Native_Router_CSRX_<release>This directory contains the combination Helm chart for both Cloud-Native Router and cSRX.

note.p: All remaining steps in the installation assume that your current working directory is now either Juniper_Cloud_Native_Router_<release> or Juniper_Cloud_Native_Router_CSRX_<release>.

step.cmd: View the contents in the current directory.
Change to the helmchart directory and expand the Helm chart.
step.info: cd helmchartls jcnr-<release>.tgztar -xzvf jcnr-<release>.tgzls jcnr jcnr-<release>.tgzThe Helm chart is located in the jcnr directory.

step.cmd: Modify the Helm chart helmchart/jcnr/values.yaml file to match the Helm chart configuration in your current installation.
Upgrade JCNR.
step.info: Navigate to the helmchart/jcnr directory and run the following command:helm upgrade jcnr .Release "jcnr" has been upgraded. Happy Helming! NAME: jcnr LAST DEPLOYED: xxxxxxx NAMESPACE: default STATUS: deployed REVISION: 2 TEST SUITE: None

step.cmd: Check that the Cloud-Native Router Helm chart is being installed.

info.codeblock: NAME NAMESPACE REVISION UPDATED STATUS CHART APP VERSION jcnr default 2 xxxxxxx deployed jcnr-xxxxxxx xxxxxxx
info.p: If the new version of Cloud-Native Router fails to install, troubleshoot the installation as you normally do. Look at the Cloud-Native Router deployer logs and see .

context.p: To downgrade or roll back from the current version to an older or previous version, uninstall the current version and install the older or previous version.

Uninstalling Cloud-Native Router restores interfaces to their original state by unbinding from DPDK and binding back to the original driver. It also deletes contents of Cloud-Native Router directories, deletes cRPD created interfaces and removes any Kubernetes objects created for JCNR. (See the restoreInterfaces attribute in the Helm chart.)

note.p: Uninstalling Cloud-Native Router using Helm does not delete the jcnr namespace or the jcnr-secrets. Delete these manually if needed.

step.cmd: Uninstall JCNR.
Wait for all Cloud-Native Router resources to be fully deleted before attempting reinstallation.
step.info: Premature re-installation can lead to installation issues and may require manual steps for recovery. If this occurs, use one or more of the following commands to clean up the uninstallation:helm uninstall jcnr --no-hooks​ kubectl delete <ds/name>​ kubectl delete <job/jobname>​ kubectl delete ns jcnrops​
